title: Balanced trees and tries

publication.changeSummary: Language-neutral DSA draft with explicit C/C++/Java/Python mapping for Balanced trees and tries; independent algorithm, implementation, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: CS2023 Algorithmic Foundations; August 2023; material applicable to Balanced trees and tries
sources.claim: Balanced search trees control height for logarithmic operations, while tries follow key symbols and trade memory for prefix operations.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Balanced search trees control height for logarithmic operations, while tries follow key symbols and trade memory for prefix operations.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A trie retrieves all words under a prefix node without comparing entire unrelated keys. Your goal is to compare balanced BST and trie suitability and implement a tested trie. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Recursive problem design; Linked lists; Binary search tree operations; String traversal and matching basics. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Shared concept and invariant
sections.body: Balanced search trees control height for logarithmic operations, while tries follow key symbols and trade memory for prefix operations. This claim is assessed independently from syntax so conceptual evidence can transfer.

sections.heading: C, C++, Java, and Python implementation map
sections.body: In C, tree nodes and heap buffers need explicit lifetime and bounds; C++ can combine RAII nodes with vector heaps; Java uses object references and PriorityQueue contracts; Python uses objects plus heapq's min-heap functions, while the conceptual invariant stays unchanged.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Balancing rotations preserve order but are implementation-specific; trie cost depends on key length and alphabet representation. The draft therefore rejects: A trie operation is O(1) because it does not depend on the number of stored words.

includes: Explain balance guarantees and implement trie insert, lookup and prefix traversal.
Compare balanced BST and trie suitability and implement a tested trie.

In C, tree nodes and heap buffers need explicit lifetime and bounds; C++ can combine RAII nodes with vector heaps; Java uses object references and PriorityQueue contracts; Python uses objects plus heapq's min-heap functions, while the conceptual invariant stays unchanged.

outcomes: Compare balanced BST and trie suitability and implement a tested trie.
